method_atom: A general interference-based quantum measurement scheme in which probe units are sent through the two arms of an interferometer and their accumulated interference signal discriminates among hidden, mutually-distinguishable internal charge sectors of a target region. A coherent superposition over the target's conserved internal charge decoheres at a rate fixed by a pairwise distinguishability (relative statistical/phase) parameter between probe and target, and repeated single-probe trials accumulate statistics, letting one estimate the number of trials required to resolve competing charge hypotheses at a chosen confidence level, with perturbative corrections when higher-order multi-passage processes contribute.
object_atom: Non-Abelian anyonic (topological) charges carried by quasiparticle excitations in two-dimensional topologically-ordered systems, realized concretely in fractional quantum Hall states — Abelian hierarchy states, the Moore-Read state at nu=5/2, and the Read-Rezayi state at nu=12/5 — probed via Mach-Zehnder and double point-contact interferometers.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is interferometric collapse of a superposed hidden conserved charge at a distinguishability-set rate plus sequential-trial statistical inference; what is domain-specific is that the charge is anyonic braiding/monodromy statistics in fractional quantum Hall systems.
